DEFENDANT WEBINAR.NET INC.'S "MOTION FOR ORDERS PURSUANT TO CALIFORNIA CODE OF CIVIL PROCEDURE SECTIONS 425.16 AND 128.5" IS DENIED WITHOUT PREJUDICE. ANTI-SLAPP MOTIONS "MAY BE FILED WITHIN 60 DAYS OF THE SERVICE OF THE COMPLAINT OR, IN THE COURT'S DISCRETION, AT ANY LATER TIME UPON TERMS IT DEEMS PROPER." (CCP 425.16(F).) COUNSEL FOR THE PREVAILING PARTY IS REQUIRED TO PREPARE A PROPOSED ORDER WHICH REPEATS VERBATIM THE SUBSTANTIVE PORTION OF THE COURT'S RULING IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312. JUDGE: ROCHELLE EAST; CLERK: W. TRUPEK; NOT REPORTED (302/RCE)

MASTER CALENDAR COURT
Off Calendar Jul-08-2025 Vacated

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Aug-28-2024 Continued To Oct-16-24 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Motion To Compel And Request For Sanctions
Off Calendar Aug-21-2024 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: RE: PLAINTIFF ON24, INC.'S Motion To Compel And Request For Sanctions.

Appearances:

Margaret A. Crawford, Esq., via Zoom videoconference, of Seubert Frimel and Warner LLP, (650) 322-3048, for Plaintiff.

Sean Healy, Esq., Law Office of, (707) 771-9660, for Defendant Webinar.net, Inc.

The parties stipulate to the matter being decided by the Judge Pro Tem. The parties and Judge Pro Tem authorize the clerk to execute the formal stipulation on their behalf.

The subject matter is argued. At the conclusion of argument, the court adopts its tentative ruling. The motion to compel is GRANTED. Plaintiff served requests for production ("Requests") and Defendant failed to timely serve responses. Accordingly, pursuant to Code of Civil Procedure section 2031.300(a), Defendant waived any objections to the Requests. The responses Defendant served on August 8, 2024, after Plaintiff filed the instant motion, improperly assert objections. Within 10 days of entry of this order, Defendant shall serve amended responses to the Requests without objections and with a Code-compliant verification. The request for sanction is DENIED as Defendant's position-i.e., its apparent belief that it had not been validly served with the summons and complaint at the time the Requests were served-was not without substantial justification.

The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must e-mail it to the Judge Pro Tem to mkahn@gibsondunn.com.

Judge Pro Tem: Matthew Kahn. Clerk: R. Woods. Not Reported. (525/JPT)

ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Jul-25-2024 Continued To Sep-11-24 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

For Order Striking 12/20/2023 Proof Of Service; Supporting Declaration And Request For Judicial Notice In Support Of Motion; Proposed Order
Off Calendar Jun-28-2024 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances via Zoom Conference
MARGARET A. CRAWFORD, Esq., SEUBERT FRIMEL AND WARNER LLP 650.322.3048 margaret@sffwlaw.com
Attorneys for Plaintiff ON24, INC for Plaintiff.
STEPHEN HEALY, Esq., LAW OFFICE OF STEPHEN HEALY (707) 772-5496 sjh@stephenhealy.com Attorney for WEBINAR.NET, INC.

Hearing is held. ARGUED. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

DEFENDANT WEBINAR.NET INC. For Order Striking 12/20/2023 Proof Of Service; Supporting
Declaration And Request For Judicial Notice In Support Of Motion; Proposed Order

Defendant Webinar.net Inc.'s "motion for orders pursuant to California Code of Civil Procedure sections 425.16 and 128.5" is denied without prejudice.

Anti-SLAPP motions "may be filed within 60 days of the service of the complaint or, in the court's discretion, at any later time upon terms it deems proper." (CCP 425.16(f).)

Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Rochelle East; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (302/RCE)

Motion For Orders Pursuant To California Code Of Civil Procedure 425.16 & 128.5; Supporting Declarations And Request For Judicial Notice In Support Of Motion
Off Calendar Jun-28-2024 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances via Zoom Conference
MARGARET A. CRAWFORD, Esq., SEUBERT FRIMEL AND WARNER LLP 650.322.3048 margaret@sffwlaw.com
Attorneys for Plaintiff ON24, INC for Plaintiff.
STEPHEN HEALY, Esq., LAW OFFICE OF STEPHEN HEALY (707) 772-5496 sjh@stephenhealy.com Attorney for WEBINAR.NET, INC.

Hearing is held. ARGUED. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

DEFENDANT WEBINAR.NET INC. Motion For Orders Pursuant To California Code Of Civil Procedure 425.16 & 128.5

Defendant Webinar.net Inc.'s motion for order striking 12/20/2023 proof of service is denied. A motion to quash is the proper vehicle by which to challenge defects in service.

Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Rochelle East; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (302/RCE)
